content.problemStatement:
There is a rod of length NNN lying on the XXX-axis with its left end at X=0X = 0X=0 and right end at X=NX = NX=N. You're given MMM points A1,A2,…,AMA_1, A_2, \dots, A_MA1​,A2​,…,AM​ in the rod such that 0<A1<A2<⋯<AM<N0 \lt A_1 \lt A_2 \lt \dots \lt A_M \lt N0<A1​<A2​<⋯<AM​<N. You have to cut the rod at all these points. You can perform these cuts in any order. You can take any segment of the rod that exists, and cut it into two smaller sub-rods. The cost of making a cut is the length of the segment of rod in which you are making the cut.

Your aim is to **minimize** this cost. Find the sequence of these MMM points in which you will make cuts. If two different sequences of cuts give the same cost, return the **lexicographically smallest** sequence.

Note: Sequence AAA is lexicographically smaller than sequence BBB if and only if there exists a position iii where Ai<BiA_i \lt B_iAi​<Bi​ and Aj=BjA_j = B_jAj​=Bj​ for all j<ij \lt ij<i.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN, MMM — the length of the rod, the number of points where you've to cut the rod.
The second line of each test case contains MMM space-separated integers A1,A2,…,AMA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_MA1​,A2​,…,AM​.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the MMM space-separated points in which order you will make cuts.
constraints: The sum of MMM over all test cases won't exceed 100010001000.

solution.approach:
This method uses recursion to divide the problem into smaller subproblems and caches (memoizes) results to avoid redundant calculations.

This approach builds the solution iteratively using a DP table instead of recursion.
